title: sapindus saponaria var. thurstonii
intro: Sapindus saponaria var. thurstonii, also known as Soapbark tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. It is a variant of the Soapbark tree (Sapindus saponaria), which is a member of the Sapindaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sapindus saponaria var. thurstonii include relief from fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and it can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
